stress.description: Stress is a physiological and psychological response to demanding circumstances, raising cortisol and tension levels.
stress.exercise: 16s Box Breathing: Inhale 4s, Hold 4s, Exhale 4s, Hold 4s.
stress.affirmation: I have the power to slow down, breathe, and tackle things one step at a time.
tips: Prioritize your tasks and delegate where possible.
Take 5-minute offline screen breaks every hour.
Engage in brief physical stretching to release muscle tension.

anxiety.description: Anxiety is characterized by persistent worry, apprehension, and autonomic nervous system hyperactivity.
anxiety.exercise: 5-4-3-2-1 Grounding: Name 5 things you see, 4 you feel, 3 you hear, 2 you smell, and 1 you taste.
anxiety.affirmation: I am safe in this present moment, and these anxious thoughts will pass.
tips: Limit caffeine and refined sugar intake.
Focus on slow, deep abdominal breathing.
Acknowledge your anxiety without judging it or fighting it.

sadness.description: Sadness is a natural emotional reaction to loss, disappointment, or feeling overwhelmed.
sadness.exercise: Gratitude Journaling: Recall and write down 3 small things that brought you comfort today.
sadness.affirmation: It is okay to feel sad. I am gentle with myself as I heal.
tips: Allow yourself to express emotions rather than bottling them up.
Connect with a supportive friend or write down your feelings.
Engage in a small, comforting self-care activity.

burnout.description: Burnout is a state of chronic physical and emotional exhaustion caused by prolonged work or life pressures.
burnout.exercise: PMR (Progressive Muscle Relaxation): Squeeze and release muscle groups sequentially.
burnout.affirmation: My worth is not defined by my productivity. I deserve rest.
tips: Establish strict boundaries between work and rest hours.
Engage in non-work related hobbies or creative outlets.
Incorporate micro-rests into your daily schedule.

overthinking.description: Overthinking involves rumination, loop-thinking, and analyzing scenarios repeatedly, raising mental fatigue.
overthinking.exercise: Brain Dump: Write down every single thought on paper to release it from your mind.
overthinking.affirmation: I release the need to control the future and trust the process of life.
tips: Set a designated 'worry time' of 10 minutes, then move on.
Distract your mind with physical activity or an engaging puzzle.
Remind yourself that thoughts are not facts.

sleep.description: Sleep issues involve difficulty falling asleep, staying asleep, or achieving restful, restorative sleep.
sleep.affirmation: My body is ready to rest, and my mind is letting go of the day.
tips: Avoid digital screens at least 1 hour before bedtime.
Keep your bedroom cool, dark, and completely quiet.
Establish a consistent sleep schedule.

confidence.description: Low confidence stems from critical self-talk, negative self-judgment, or comparing oneself to others.
confidence.exercise: Strengths Inventory: List 3 personal qualities or past challenges you successfully overcame.
confidence.affirmation: I am competent, capable, and worthy of growth and success.
tips: Catch and reframe self-deprecating thoughts immediately.
Celebrate small wins and accept compliments graciously.
Set small, achievable daily goals to build momentum.

motivation.description: Motivation issues occur due to lack of clarity, emotional exhaustion, or feeling overwhelmed by a task.
motivation.exercise: The 5-Minute Rule: Commit to working on a task for just 5 minutes, then decide if you want to stop.
motivation.affirmation: I don't need to do everything at once. I just need to take the first small step.
tips: Break large goals into micro-tasks.
Identify the 'why' behind what you want to achieve.
Reward yourself for completing small tasks.
